body:
### Problem

ref: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/addd408b3955a16ebecb7972d6a12a91711ea108/src/nvim/terminal.c#L2502

Neovim can abort in terminal refresh when `adjust_scrollback()` runs while `sb_pending > 0`

This happens very commonly when resuming large (~10MB) sessions in  `codex`, which suddenly fire too many events to the terminal.
This issue is very rare with native `:terminal`, but with a decorated terminal like `Snacks.terminal`, this issue happens almost every time.

NOTE:
* It works fine if I change the `abort()` to `return;`
* I traced to this line from the crash dump.

#### Coredump
<details>
<summary>

## Click to see output of `coredump info nvim`

</summary>

```

                #5  0x00007f7b10f103d0 __clone3 (libc.so.6 + 0x1103d0)
                ELF object binary architecture: AMD x86-64
```
</details>

### Steps to reproduce

* Install https://github.com/folke/snacks.nvim/
* Open `lua Snacks.terminal({'codex', 'resume'})`
* Enter a large session. I picked a session which is >10MB in `.codex/sessions/`
* Observe: the terminal rapidly scrolls, but Neovim crashes with SIGABRT

### Expected behavior

No crash, like every other terminal.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

0.12.0-dev-2586+g16f7440cc7

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

not tried

### Operating system/version

RHEL 9.7

### Terminal name/version

Windows terminal -> WSL -> SSH into RHEL machine -> tmux -> nvim

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-256color

### Installation

Github releases ( installed using `mise`

labels.description: issue reporting a crash or segfault
labels.name: bug-crash

title: terminal: SIGABRT when scrollback is still pending during terminal refresh
